claim: Hand B exhibits vowel harmony: stem-internal vowel composition (front {i,e} vs back {o,a}) predicts the cluster membership of stems based on which vowel-class Layer-1 inner morphemes they preferentially take. Sub-test 1c of H-BV-HAND-B-FULL-BATTERY-01.
evidence_for: Hand A's H-BV-VOWEL-HARMONY-01 was REFUTED at silhouette 0.33 (just below 0.35 threshold) on 73 eligible stems. Hand B has more tokens (~23,700 vs ~11,000), giving more eligible stems and higher statistical power. If Hand A's near-threshold silhouette was under-powered, Hand B might cross the threshold.

Hand B and Hand A share the same V/C sub-class structure (H-BV-HAND-B-SUFFIX-SUBCLASS-01 sub-test 1b just confirmed n vowel-exclusivity in Hand B). If vowel harmony exists in the manuscript, Hand B should show it.

Locking the test as exact methodology replication of Hand A VOWEL-HARMONY-01 with only the corpus filter changed maintains pre-registration discipline and methodology lesson 9 (parallel testing on companion corpora — do not assume extension).
evidence_against: Hand A's silhouette was 0.33, very close to the 0.35 REFUTED threshold but not at it. The clustering structure is weak; more data may not push silhouette dramatically higher.

Vowel harmony as a typological feature is associated with Turkic and Finno-Ugric languages — both of which were ruled out as candidates by H-BV-VOWEL-HARMONY-01's REFUTED verdict on Hand A. The non-harmonic finding led directly to narrowing the AGGLUTINATIVE-EXPANSION candidate set to non-harmonic languages.
Hand B's section-coupled vocabulary may introduce stem-internal vowel patterns that confound the harmony test (sections might use different stem vowel patterns for content reasons).
test:
LOCKED PROCEDURE — IDENTICAL METHODOLOGY TO H-BV-VOWEL-HARMONY-01 with corpus filter changed from currier_language=='A' to 'B'.

  DATA: Hand B tokens under Tokenisation A (MULTI_GLYPHS + 'ol'), N>=3 glyph-units.

  DEFINITIONS (LOCKED, identical to Hand A test):
    VOWEL-INNERS = {i, e, o, a}
    FRONT-VOWELS = {i, e}
    BACK-VOWELS  = {o, a}
    STEM = all glyph-units before the final two

  STEP 1 STEM POOL: Eligible stems = >=3 distinct vowel-inner attachments AND >=5 total vowel-inner tokens. LOW-POWER if <50.
  STEP 2 FEATURE VECTORS: 4-dim L1-normalised distribution over {i,e,o,a} per eligible stem.
  STEP 3 CLUSTERING: K-means k=2, 50 restarts, Euclidean distance. Silhouette score.
  STEP 4 LABELLING: Cluster with larger mean p(i)+p(e) -> FRONT; other -> BACK.
  STEP 5 PREDICTION: For each stem, count front and back vowels inside. Predict FRONT or BACK from majority. Compute accuracy vs cluster label.

  DECISION RULE (LOCKED, identical to Hand A):
    CONFIRMED vowel harmony: silhouette >= 0.50 AND accuracy >= 0.70
    CONFIRMED two-class non-harmonic: silhouette >= 0.50 AND accuracy < 0.70
    REFUTED: silhouette < 0.35
    MARGINAL: silhouette in [0.35, 0.50] OR (silhouette >= 0.50 AND accuracy in [0.60, 0.70))
    LOW-POWER if eligible <50.
test_metric: silhouette score on k-means k=2 over stems-by-vowel-distribution, plus stem-internal-vowel prediction accuracy.
test_threshold: CONFIRMED if silhouette>=0.50 AND accuracy>=0.70. REFUTED if silhouette<0.35. MARGINAL otherwise.

tests_run.interpretation: MAJOR cross-hand divergence. Hand A was REFUTED for vowel harmony at silhouette 0.33; Hand B CONFIRMS vowel harmony at silhouette 0.65 (nearly 2x Hand A's value) AND prediction accuracy 0.77 (above the 0.70 threshold). The cluster structure is sharply bimodal: Cluster 0 is strongly a-preferring (mean p(a)=0.68, p(e)=0.14), Cluster 1 is strongly e-preferring (mean p(e)=0.69, p(a)=0.13). Stem-internal vowel composition predicts cluster membership at 77% accuracy — exactly the signature of vowel harmony. This is a SHARP STRUCTURAL DIFFERENCE between hands and contradicts a unified-morphology interpretation. Implications: (a) Hand A and Hand B may encode different linguistic systems, OR (b) the same language with hand-specific scribal conventions, OR (c) different content domains using different vocabulary patterns where Hand B's more diverse section composition reveals harmony Hand A's heavily herbal sample doesn't.

reasoning: Sub-test 1c of the systematic Hand B replication. Hand A was REFUTED at silhouette 0.33; Hand B has ~2x token count and may cross the 0.35 threshold or push toward higher silhouette. The locked rule replicates Hand A's exactly. Result determines whether the non-harmonic conclusion is manuscript-wide or hand-specific.
